evidence_quotes.quote: Videogames are far more than just a simple product: They are a huge market bringing in billions in revenue.

paragraphs.text: Videogames are far more than just a simple product: They are a huge market bringing in billions in revenue. With this, of course, comes corporate greed, which plumets the quality of their products. This didn't happen overnight, and they have shamelessly stepped over consumer rights. They destroy innovation, they use scammy, excuse my lack of better terms, and they will not stop until someone makes them. STOP KILLING GAMES is a great initiative to stop publishers from destroying their product after consumers have bought it, yes, but I would like to add one thing: Patents A famous example is the Nemesis System, featured in Middle-earth: Shadow of Mordor and Shadow of War, is currently locked away by a patent held by Warner Bros. until 2036, which prevents other studios from using or developing similar systems without permission. In my opinion, its like pattening a color, and refusing other artists to draw with it. There is also another example, with Namco: They held a prominent patent (US 5718632 A) that restricted other companies from implementing auxiliary mini-games during game loading screens from 1995 until its expiration in November 2015, and by 2015, that system was useless, due to the short loading screen time. We need patents, yes, but they need to be reasonable
